ksa_text: Ability to interpret and apply laws, regulations, policies, and organizational guidance in specific operational contexts.

dcwf.top_element_label: Ability to interpret and apply laws, regulations, policies, and guidance relevant to organization cyber objectives.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Ability to interpret and apply laws, regulations, policies, and guidance relevant to organization cyber objectives.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Regulatory Compliance falls outside the primary competency scope of the Data and AI Research Scientist. This ability KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is Regulatory Compliance truly out-of-scope for the Data and AI Research Scientist? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list Regulatory Compliance as a core or recommended competency for the Data and AI Research Scientist. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms Regulatory Compliance falls outside the established competency profile for the Data and AI Research Scientist. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Regulatory Compliance is outside the competency scope of the Data and AI Research Scientist. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
